name: Gradient Ventures
thesis: Backing AI-native B2B tools with a clear distribution wedge and a founder who's shipped before.
bio: Led 12 pre-seed rounds in AI tooling. Former PM at Stripe. Exits: 2 acquisitions, 1 IPO.
reasons: Strong ICP fit in the AI tooling category your product competes in
Your MRR trajectory matches their portfolio median at this stage

name: Sequoia Arc
thesis: Founders who've identified a non-obvious wedge in a large market and can articulate the 10-year vision.
bio: Partner at Sequoia Arc. Previously backed 4 unicorns in B2B infrastructure. Hands-on with GTM for Seed-stage companies.
reasons: Infrastructure play with recurring revenue characteristics
Founder background aligns with their operator-first thesis

name: Pioneer Fund
thesis: Products with organic viral loops and communities that grow without a paid acquisition budget.
bio: Angel investor, 8 bets. Ex-founder (exited to Meta). Writing weekly on network effects and growth.
investmentThesis: Organic-first growth with network effect ceiling
reasons: Viral coefficient and referral mechanics match their portfolio playbook
Community-first product thesis aligns with their investment lens

name: Operator Collective
thesis: Operators backing operators. We invest in founders who've done the job the product is solving.
bio: Collective of 40+ operators from Salesforce, HubSpot, and Notion. Syndicate-based investing with active GTM support.
investmentThesis: Founder-market fit as the primary investment signal
reasons: Founder background in the problem domain creates unfair insight
Ops-tech category is a core thesis for this collective

name: Hustle Fund
thesis: Fast to decide. Backing builders who ship, learn, and pivot faster than anyone else in the market.
bio: Partner at Hustle Fund. Over 500 investments in 18 countries. Speed and capital efficiency are the only signals that matter.
investmentThesis: Capital efficiency and shipping velocity over everything
reasons: Capital efficiency profile matches Hustle Fund's default alive thesis
Iteration speed signals from your product timeline are in their sweet spot

name: South Park Commons
thesis: The most interesting companies start as communities. We back founders still figuring out what to build.
bio: Community director at SPC. Background in developer communities and OSS. Connects portfolio to 1,200+ active builders.
reasons: Developer community traction is a strong signal for their portfolio thesis
Technical depth of your team aligns with their preferred founder profile
